CONSTITUTION AND BYLAWS

OF THE

SOUTHEAST LOUISIANA ASSOCIATION OF DISABILTY EXAMINERS

A CHAPTER OF THE

NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF DISABILITY EXAMINERS, INC.

ARTICLE I

NAME

The name of this professional and non-profit organization shall be the Southeast Louisiana Association of Disability Examiners. It is officially affiliated with the National Association of Disability Examiner, Inc.

ARTICLE II

RELATIONSHIP TO THE NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF DISABILITY EXAMINERS

The Southeast Louisiana Association of Disability Examiners is a professional chapter of the National Association of Disability Examiners, Inc. and is subject to the provisions of the constitution and by-laws pertaining to N.A.D.E.

ARTICLE III

DEFINITIONS

For the purpose of these by-laws:

ASSOCIATION means the National Association of Disability Examiners.

REGION means Southwest Region, a geographical unit of the National Association of Disability Examiners consisting of the states of Louisiana, Arkansas, Oklahoma, Texas, and New Mexico.

CHAPTER means S.L.A.D.E., a professional unit of N.A.D.E.

BOARD means the Board of Directors of S.L.A.D.E.

ARTICLE IV

PURPOSES

The purpose of the S.L.A.D.E. is to project the professional functions and duties of disability examiners on both a state-wide and national level in the evaluation and documentation of disabilities by:

1. Increasing public understanding of disability programs.

2. Developing professional standards and training opportunities for all individuals and/or adjudication of disability claims.

3. Improving the documentation of applications for disability benefits and the evaluation of medical and vocational information obtained in connection with such applications.

4. Providing a form for the discussion of problems related to documentation and/or adjudication of disability claims.

ARTICLE V

MEMBERSHIP

All members of the National Association of Disability Examiners residing or employed in the Southeastern section of the State of Louisiana which is serviced by the New Orleans area office.

The membership and fiscal year of the Chapter shall be the same as those designated by the Association.

ARTICLE VI

LIMITATIONS

The limitations upon Chapter activities in regard to propaganda, campaigning for any candidate for public office or any other activities not permitted by the designated Tax Exemption Status shall be the same as those designated by the Association.

ARTICLE VII

MEETINGS

There shall be at least one meeting of the membership each year. The President may call other meetings as may be necessary, or additional meetings may be called upon written request of at least 25% of the membership.

Notices of the time and place of the regular and special meetings of the membership shall be given by the Secretary at least ten days prior to the meeting date.

Voting on business issues will be open unless a motion for a secret ballot is made and passed by majority vote of those present. All balloting for officers will be held by secret ballot and majority votes will carry all issues.

ARTICLE VIII

ELECTION

Election of officers and of directors shall be held at the Chapter’s annual meeting. The terms of the newly elected officers and directors shall begin with the month of October.

ARTICLE IX

OFFICERS

The officers of this chapter shall be a President, President-Elect, Secretary, and Treasurer. At the Chapter’s annual meeting, the President-Elect, the Secretary and the Treasurer shall be elected for terms of one year to begin with the month of October.

The duties of the officers shall be those usually pertaining to such positions including the following specific duties assigned to each office:

1. The President shall preside at all meetings and as Chairman of the Board; shall appoint committees and exercise supervision of the affairs of the Chapter; shall aid in coordinating the work of this Chapter with that of the parent organization.

2. The President-Elect shall preside in the absence of the President; shall be responsible for the supervision of the programs and committees of the Chapter; shall serve as the representative to the Regional Executive Committee.

3. The Secretary shall record upon the books the records of the Chapter the proceedings of their respective meetings; shall notify all members and directors of meetings; shall perform the duties incident to the office of Secretary.

4. The Treasurer shall have custody of all monies belonging to the Chapter and shall disburse the same pursuant to the orders of the Board or Chapter; shall make current financial reports at all meetings of the Board and the Chapter; shall have other duties from time to time as assigned by the President or by the Board.

ARTICLE X

BOARD OF DIRECTORS

Composition of the Board of Directors: The Board shall consist of the four officers of the Chapter and two members of the chapter.

The Board shall be elected by the membership at the annual meeting of the Chapter. Terms of this office will be for two years provided, however, that of the original two members of the Chapter, one shall be elected for a term of one year and the other for two years. No Director may succeed himself unless he is elected as an officer of the Chapter.

At least thirty days in advance of the annual business meeting, the membership shall be advised of the names of persons nominated for officers and for the Board. At the annual business meeting, additional nominations may be made from the floor.

The Board shall conduct, in conformity with these By-laws, the affairs of the Chapter when it is not in session. Four members of the Board shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business.

1. It shall have the power to authorize expenditures of money.

2. It shall have the power to authorize the President to appoint committees to carry out the purposes of the Chapter.

3. It shall have the power to fill vacancies in its own body until the next meeting of the Chapter.

4. It shall have the power to make and amend the rules for its own procedures. It shall keep a record of such rules.

5. It shall take steps as may be necessary to carry out a program or function determined by vote of the members and not otherwise provided for.

6. It shall make a continuing study of techniques and recommend plans for the improvement of performance or professional personnel.

7. The Chairman of the Board shall be empowered to call special meetings of the Board as the need may appear or shall call such special meetings upon the written request of two Board members.

The fiscal affairs of the Chapter shall be audited annually as of September 30 by three members appointed from the membership by the Board.

ARTICLE XI

COMMITTEES

There shall be four standing committees as follows: Resolutions and By-Laws, Nominating, Conference and Program, and Awards and Citations.

All members shall be appointed for one year and are eligible for reappointment but may serve no more than three consecutive terms.

1. The Resolution and By-laws Committee is empowered to study the Constitution and By-laws for possible revision or amendment; to receive and process suggestions for revisions of amendments; to present recommendations; to originate, receive and process resolutions for presentation at the business session of the Chapter’s meeting.

2. The Nominating Committee shall prepare a slate of nominees. The President shall have the proposed slate of nominees sent to the membership body of the chapter at least thirty days prior to the date of the business session of the annual meeting. The names of two members who will have agreed to serve if elected shall be presented for each office.

3. The Conference and Program Committee shall plan and arrange for Chapter meetings; shall recommend a program of activity for the Chapter.

4. The Awards and Citation Committee shall consider and select the recipients of any awards and/or citations.

5. The Board may appoint such other committees, particularly ad hoc, as it deems necessary.

ARTICLE XII

PARLIAMENTARY AUTHORITY

Robert’s Rules of Order: The most recent edition shall be the authority on all questions of Parliamentary Law and proceedings. This authority shall govern the Chapter in all cases to which the rules therein are applicable and in which said rules are not inconsistent with the By-laws.

ARTICLE XIII

AMENDMENT OF CONSTITUTION AND BY-LAWS

The Constitution and By-laws may be amended by a majority of those members attending the Chapter meeting, providing notices of the business meetings indicating that amendments to the By-laws will be considered as part of the business have been sent to the members at least ten days in advance of the meeting.

ARTICLE XIV

CONVENTION OF DELEGATES

The President or President-Elect may attend by virtue of the offices they hold. In the event the President or President-Elect is unable to attend, the Board shall name a delegate.

Remaining delegates must be of the membership and shall be proposed by the Board.

Proposed delegates shall be submitted by the Board to the membership body of the Chapter at regular business sessions at least sixty days prior to the annual convention. The Board shall propose one delegate for each position open. Additional nominations may be made from the floor. Final selection of delegates shall then by made by the Board.

A written proxy may be utilized when carried by the delegate or delegates to the convention.
